name: Burnt-Orange Meringue Cake
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Meringue Layers:
1. Pulse the almonds in a food processor until coarsely ground.
2. Spread on a baking pan and toast for about 15 minutes, stirring frequently, until golden.
3. Cool and combine with sugar and cornstarch.
4. Reduce oven to 300°F.
5. Whip egg whites on medium speed with salt until white and opaque.
6. Increase speed to maximum and whip in remaining sugar.
7. Continue whipping until egg whites hold a firm peak.
8. Fold in extracts and toasted almond mixture.
9. Pipe batter onto paper-lined pans using a pastry bag fitted with a 1/2-inch plain tube to make three 10-inch disks.
10. Bake at 300°F for about 30 minutes, until crisp but not completely dry.
11. Cool layers on pans.
Caramel-Orange Filling:
1. Combine sugar and lemon juice in a saucepan; stir well.
2. Heat on medium, stirring occasionally, until sugar melts and caramelizes.
3. Carefully add milk (the caramel will splatter) and return to boil, stirring to dissolve any hardened caramel.
4. Whisk yolks with orange zest in a bowl.
5. Whisk half the boiling milk into yolk mixture.
6. Return remaining milk to boil and whisk in yolk mixture until thickened.
7. Strain into a bowl and cool.
Glazed Orange Slices:
1. Combine orange juice and gelatin in a bowl; bring to soft consistency.
2. Heat apricot preserves over low heat, stirring occasionally, until boiling.
3. Add orange mixture and return to boil slightly.
4. Cool slightly and dip orange slices thinly into glaze.
5. Place on a pan lined with plastic wrap to set.
Assembly:
1. Trim layers to an even 10-inch diameter and place one layer on cardboard.
2. Spread filling over the first layer.
3. Repeat with second layer and fill center circle with remaining filling.
4. Top with last layer and spread remainder of filling.
5. Use remaining filling to make a border of rosettes around the edge of the cake and in the center.
6. Press toasted almonds against the side of the cake and fill the center with them.
Orange Slices:
Arrange orange slices on rosettes.
